messages.content:
When creating a waterfall chart for ABC Inc.’s 2018 net earnings, which of the data points do you need to set as totals? Select ALL correct answers.



Taxes
Gross profit
Cost of goods sold
Interest
Net earnings
Salaries & benefits
Revenue
Depreciation & amortization
Net earnings

Which charts would be better choices to visualize a company's actual performance ratio in comparison to a target ratio? Select TWO best answers.

Area chart
Scatter chart with lines
Two-series line chart
Column and marker chart
Pie-chart

Two-series line chart
Column and marker chart

Which of the following combinations will allow you to align multiple items to the left?

Home -> Paragraph -> Align -> Align Left
View -> Drawing -> Arrange –> Align -> Align Left
Home -> Drawing -> Arrange -> Align -> Align Left
Home -> Drawing -> Align
Home -> Drawing -> Arrange -> Align -> Align Left

The highlighted pane in orange in the screenshot below is known as the




Notes section
Slide pane
Navigation pane
Placeholder
Slide pane

What are financial modeling guidelines and why are they relevant?


A stringent set of rules about financial modeling, which are required for Microsoft Excel to function properly.
A set of rules about financial models issued by the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB). They are required for financial reporting purposes.
A set of principles and guidance for financial modeling which promote consistency across models used by all stakeholders.
A list of optional keyboard shortcuts which speed up the process of financial modeling.
A set of principles and guidance for financial modeling which promote consistency across models used by all stakeholders.

Why would an increase in the company’s days in A/P produce cash for a business?

The company is ‘stretching its payables’ and deferring a cash outflow.
The company is collecting on its credit sales faster.
The company is selling more product at a quicker pace, and therefore experiencing cash inflows.
This is a non-cash item on the balance sheet, and therefore not applicable.
The company is ‘stretching its payables’ and deferring a cash outflow.

Why would an increase in a businesses’ days in inventory consume cash?


This is a non-cash item on the balance sheet, and therefore not applicable.
The business is expending cash to build up its inventory levels.
The company is collecting on its credit sales faster.
The market is becoming increasingly competitive.
The business is expending cash to build up its inventory levels.

A real-estate holdings company has the following fixed asset detail:

Buildings: $25.0 Million; Useful Life 20-years.
Land: $5.0 Million.

Assuming straight-line depreciation, what would the company’s total annual depreciation expense be?


$5.0 million.
$1.25 million.
$30.0 million.
$20.0 million.

$1.25 million. 

Annual Depreciation Expenses = (Cost Of Asset - Salvage Value)/Useful Life

Buildings Depreciation Expenses = ($25.0 million - $0)/20-years = $1.25 million
Land has an indefinite useful life and is not depreciable.

The Role of Financial Modeling Professionals
Financial modeling professionals are expected to perform many tasks.
Which of the following is not typically a task as part of the financial modeling process?

Smoothing data for management
Reports for evaluating a business or investment
Setup for presentation and printing
Check model integrity
Smoothing data for management.

Financial Modeling Practices
Financial modeling professionals typically break their work down into modular steps for ease and transparency, before linking everything back to the three core financial statements.
Corkscrew schedules are primarily used to support calculations for which financial statement?

Cash Flow Statement
Income Statement
Statement of Operations
Balance Sheet
Cash Flow Statement.

Circularity in models
Many sophisticated models use circular calculations.
What is a required Excel setting that allows for circular calculations?


Enable Live Preview
Use table names in formulas
Enable iterative calculations
Automatic calculations
Enable iterative calculations.

DCF Schedule – Important Dates
In order to properly discount cash flows to the present value, we must consider the timing of when those cash flows occur.
Which of the following is NOT one of the important dates when creating a DCF valuation?


Fiscal Year End
Valuation Date
Reporting Date
Cash Flow Timing
Cash Flow Timing.
